cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta G = \Delta G^\circ + RT\ln Q$ relating free energy to standard free energy and reaction quotient
cards.answer: Gibbs free energy at non-standard conditions

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta G^\circ = -RT\ln K$ at equilibrium ($\Delta G = 0$, $Q = K$)
cards.answer: Standard Gibbs free energy and equilibrium constant relationship

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An electrochemical cell that converts spontaneous chemical energy into electrical energy

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An electrochemical cell that uses electrical energy to drive a non-spontaneous reaction

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The electrode where oxidation occurs in an electrochemical cell (mnemonic: AN OX)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The electrode where reduction occurs in an electrochemical cell (mnemonic: RED CAT)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $E^\circ_{\text{cell}} = E^\circ_{\text{cathode}} - E^\circ_{\text{anode}}$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The standard electrode potential measured relative to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE = 0 V)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ_{\text{cell}}$ where $n$ = moles of electrons, $F$ = Faraday's constant (96,485 C/mol)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Faraday's constant ($F$)
cards.answer: $F = 96{,}485$ C/mol $\approx 96{,}500$ C/mol; the charge of one mole of electrons

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $E = E^\circ - \frac{RT}{nF}\ln Q = E^\circ - \frac{0.0592}{n}\log Q$ at 25°C

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A porous barrier or tube containing an ionic solution that maintains electrical neutrality in a galvanic cell

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A positive $E^\circ_{\text{cell}}$ indicates the reaction is...
cards.answer: Spontaneous ($\Delta G^\circ < 0$); the galvanic cell produces useful work

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The process by which metals corrode through electrochemical oxidation (e.g., iron rusting)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A method of protecting metals from corrosion by connecting them to a more active metal (the sacrificial anode)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $m = \frac{MIt}{nF}$ where $m$ = mass deposited, $M$ = molar mass, $I$ = current (A), $t$ = time (s)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: In a galvanic cell using $\text{Zn}|\text{Zn}^{2+}$ and $\text{Cu}^{2+}|\text{Cu}$, the anode is...
cards.answer: $\text{Zn}$ (oxidized: $\text{Zn} \rightarrow \text{Zn}^{2+} + 2e^-$); zinc has a lower reduction potential than copper

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Electrochemical deposition of a metal layer onto a surface by passing current through an ionic solution

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: For a spontaneous cell reaction, $\Delta G^\circ$ is _____, $E^\circ_{\text{cell}}$ is _____, and $K > 1$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The temperature at which a reaction changes from spontaneous to non-spontaneous ($\Delta G = 0$)
